title: Content Telemetry Session
description: Schema for Content Telemetry sessions - tracking content usage in AI agent interactions

document_type.description: Document type discriminator. 'session' for session documents. When absent, consumers SHOULD treat the document as a session.

conformance_level.description: Informational conformance level advertised by the emitter. The authoritative level is declared in the emitter's manifest (section 8). See section 5.7.

content_scope.description: Opaque content collection identifier (e.g., manifest URL, API key scope, agreement ID)

manifest_ref.description: Manifest reference - the URL of a manifest at /.well-known/content-telemetry.json. See section 8.

events.description: Ordered list of events in the session (chronological by timestamp)

TelemetryEvent.description: Single telemetry event within a session

turn_id.description: Associates this event with a conversation turn. Scoped to the session. SHOULD be set on turn_started, turn_completed, content_cited, content_displayed, content_engaged events, and content_grounded events when scope is turn.

source_role.description: Who is reporting this event (see section 4.4). SHOULD be set on content_retrieved events.

content_telemetry_id.description: Correlation ID from the Content-Telemetry-ID HTTP header, used to deduplicate retrieval events reported by multiple observers

content_url.description: Content URL as fetched or canonical URL

content_id.description: Stable content identifier (CMS ID, DOI, ISBN, ISCC, C2PA manifest hash, or marketplace catalogue ID). See section 4.5.

license_ref.description: Reference to the content access licence (JWT jti, CoMP package ID, or opaque identifier)

evidence.description: Evidence for precisely scoped assertions in this event. Tiers classify one assertion, never the event, session, emitter, or deployment as a whole (section 5.8).

data.description: Type-specific metadata. See section 6 of the specification for data profiles by event type and source role.

excerpt_hash.description: SHA-256 of the cited excerpt text as it appears in the response (sha256:{hex})

content_hash.description: SHA-256 matching the corresponding content_grounded event. When the agent chunked the source, this is the chunk hash.

bot_category.description: Edge platform's bot classification. Recommended values: training, inference, search

bot_name.description: Recognised bot family parsed from the User-Agent (e.g., Claude-User, GPTBot, Perplexity-User). Stable across product variants within a vendor.

cache_status.description: Edge cache result. Recommended values: hit, miss, bypass, dynamic

EventType.description: Core event types. Extensions may define additional types such as checkout_completed.

response_mode.description: Product surface or generation mode (e.g., 'standard', 'deep_research'). See section 5.4.1.

content_urls_retrieved.description: Content URLs fetched to answer the query

ad_rendered.description: Whether advertising was displayed alongside the response

PrivacyLevel.description: Privacy levels for conversation data sharing

IntentCategory.description: Intent category. Core values: question, explanation, comparison, how_to, troubleshooting, fact_check, analysis, opinion_seeking, creative, purchase_intent, chitchat, other. Extensions MAY define additional values such as price_check, availability_check, review_seeking. Telemetry consumers MUST tolerate unknown values.

ResponseMode.description: Product surface or generation mode. Recommended values: standard, deep_research, search, code_generation. Platforms MAY use custom values for additional product surfaces.

SourceRole.description: Who is reporting the event. Origin: content owner's web server. Edge: CDN or edge network. Index: search index or content repository. Agent: the AI agent itself.

SourceProvenance.description: How the grounded content reached the agent's context (see section 6.4). 'agent_fetched': the agent retrieved the content directly from the content owner in this session. 'agent_cached': the agent reuses content it fetched in an earlier session. 'third_party_sourced': the content reached the agent via an intermediary (e.g. a marketplace, a scraper-resale vendor, or any party other than the content owner), so no content_retrieved event against the content owner exists for it. Agent-observable: the agent knows which of these applies. Disambiguates the 'cached' boolean, which does not distinguish a self-cache from third-party-sourced content.

ContentFingerprint.description: An agent-reported claim that a publisher-side provenance mark was checked in grounded content. Scheme properties describe capability ceilings only; they do not prove an instance or establish an effective evidence tier (sections 5.8 and 6.4).

scheme.description: Identifier of the provenance scheme checked by the agent (free string; not a closed enum).

detected.description: Agent-reported claim that a mark of the named scheme was found. Evidence for this assertion is carried in the containing event's evidence array.

preserved_in_output.description: Agent-reported claim that the detected mark remained recoverable in a generated output. Evidence must bind both grounded content and generated output.

scheme_profile_ref.description: Absolute, immutable or version-specific scheme profile identifier. Registry presence does not confer trust or a tier.

scheme_profile_digest.description: SHA-256 of the referenced scheme profile bytes.

SchemeProperties.description: Scheme capability ceiling. All three core properties true make a fingerprint assertion eligible to request independently_verifiable; any false value caps it at origin_corroborated. A consumer still verifies the digest-bound profile and instance evidence.

content_borne.description: The verification signal is carried with the content rather than only in detachable side metadata.

identity_bearing.description: Successful verification can bind the subject to an identified issuer under a trust policy.

verifiable.description: The profile defines a public verification procedure and interoperable outcomes.

EvidenceTier.description: Emitter-requested classification for one assertion. Consumers derive the effective tier under section 5.8.

role.description: Subject role. Core values include grounded_content, generated_output, generation_context, and event.

digest.description: SHA-256 of exact subject bytes under the profile's canonicalisation rules.

type.description: Core values: origin_event, access_record, verifier_attestation, verification_material, trusted_timestamp. Consumers tolerate extension values.

digest.description: SHA-256 of decoded inline bytes or retrieved response-body bytes, never base64 or URI text.

uri.description: Absolute immutable or version-specific reference; consumers apply safe retrieval policy and verify the digest.

content.description: Base64-encoded artifact bytes when carried inline; at most 8 MiB after decoding.

ref.description: Absolute immutable or version-specific public verification procedure identifier.

digest.description: SHA-256 of the verification profile bytes.

scheme_profile_digest.description: For fingerprint assertions, digest of the separate scheme profile this evidence profile verifies.

id.description: Absolute policy or trust-anchor identifier compared against consumer-preconfigured local trust.

digest.description: SHA-256 binding the immutable policy or trust-anchor material/version.

verifier.description: Absolute verifier implementation/service and version identifier; not by itself a trust assertion.

verified_at.description: Time verification ran; not a trusted timestamp.

reason_code.description: Open reason code; consumers tolerate unknown values.

verifier_attestation_digest.description: Digest selecting exactly one verifier_attestation artifact that binds the assertion digest and subjects.

trusted_timestamp_digest.description: Digest selecting exactly one trusted_timestamp artifact whose message imprint covers the verifier attestation or signed assertion envelope.

assertion_path.description: RFC 6901 JSON Pointer resolving within the containing event; it cannot target evidence.

assertion_digest.description: SHA-256 of the RFC 8785 occurrence-bound assertion binding defined in section 5.8.

CitationType.description: How content was used in the response

MediaType.description: Content medium. Core values: text, image, video, audio. Emitters MAY use custom values for media outside the core set (e.g. 3d, dataset). Consumers MUST tolerate unknown values.

EngagementType.description: How the user acted on content. Core values: link_click, expand, copy, share, agent_navigate. Extensions MAY define additional values (e.g. commerce actions). Consumers MUST tolerate unknown values.

GroundingScope.description: Whether content informed all subsequent responses in the session or a specific turn only

DisplayType.description: How content or a content reference was presented to the user. Core values: link, snippet, inline_quote, card, detail_view, embed. Platforms MAY use custom values for additional presentation surfaces. Consumers MUST tolerate unknown values.
